name: Crescent Chick-be-Quicks
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup crushed canned French fried onions
8 ounces Pillsbury Refrig Quick Crescent Rolls
1 whole chicken breast, skinned/boned - cut into 16 pieces (about 1/2 pound)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease a cookie sheet lightly.
3. In a small bowl, mix together French fried onions, flour, and seasoned salt until well combined. Set aside.
4. Unroll the dough and cut it into 8 triangles. Cut each triangle in half diagonally to form 2 smaller triangles.
5. Dip chicken pieces into the beaten egg, then coat with the onion mixture.
6. Place one coated chicken piece on the wide end of each triangle, rolling them up to the opposite point.
7. Lay the rolls point-side down on the prepared cookie sheet.
8. Brush the tops of the rolls with the remaining beaten egg and sprinkle with sesame or poppy seeds.
9. Bake for 11-13 minutes, or until golden brown. Serve warm or cold.
10. Makes 16 servings.
- Per serving: 90 calories, 4 grams protein, 7 grams carbohydrate, 5 grams fat, 25 milligrams cholesterol, 160 milligrams sodium
- Pillsbury Classic Cookbook #86, 33rd Bake-Off, p.8
- Submitted by Rosemarie Berger, Jamestown, NC
